[suggestion] [info] Zone "Project Root" (project-root) has at-risk risk (score: 0.50, cohesion: 0.00, coupling: 0.00) — approaching architectural risk thresholds

<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 9 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- context.md and llms.txt are generated outputs from sourcevision — they should not be edited manually and should be regenerated after significant structural changes.
- vitest.config.ts sits at the root but all test code lives in packages/; verify the config correctly scopes test discovery to avoid false positives.
- Rex-F.png and Rex.png are binary assets in the root; consider moving them to a docs/ or assets/ directory to keep the root clean.
- Zero cohesion and coupling are expected — root contains only config and documentation files with no import relationships.
- AI context files (context.md, llms.txt) appear alongside source config; a dedicated .sourcevision/ output directory (already used elsewhere) would make the root less cluttered.
- context.md and llms.txt appear in both the project root and .sourcevision/ — duplicate generated artifact locations create ambiguity about which copy is authoritative and which consumers should read.
- Duplicate generated output locations (root context.md vs .sourcevision/CONTEXT.md) create a two-source-of-truth problem; tooling that reads the wrong copy will silently operate on stale data.
- vitest.config.ts is zone-assigned to project-root alongside static assets and documentation, but it is a runtime test orchestrator that discovers and executes code across all packages. Its zone placement in a data/config zone obscures its role as a global orchestration entry point — the most consequential config file in the root is treated equivalently to Rex.png.
- vitest.config.ts has fundamentally different architectural significance from other project-root zone members (README.md, Rex.png, package.json). Metrics that average across this zone conflate passive static files with an active cross-package orchestrator. Sourcevision should exclude vitest.config.ts from cohesion/coupling calculations for this zone, or assign it to a dedicated test-config zone alongside any per-package vitest configs.

</insights>
